summary.intent: Maintain the moltis project by fixing a biome formatting failure, cutting a new release (20260328.03), responding to a GitHub discussion about forum topic isolation, and fixing a failing e2e auth test.
summary.outcome: All four goals completed: biome fix committed and pushed, release 20260328.03 tagged and pushed, discussion comment posted, and the flaky e2e auth test fixed and committed.

repo: Release tags follow date-based format (e.g. 20260328.03); the prepare script auto-increments the patch number and updates the changelog.
local-validate.sh runs parallel checks (biome, fmt, i18n, install-names, zizmor, e2e) and reports aggregate pass/fail.

The session header (chat-session-name span) is mounted twice: once in the toolbar (showName=false, hidden) and once inside the #chatMoreModal (hidden by default) — getByText('Main chat') matches both hidden instances.
The sidebar session item uses data-session-key attribute and a [data-label-text] span showing session.label || session.key.
Forum topic isolation was implemented via per-topic session keys, composite chat_id:thread_id addressing, interrupt isolation, explicit topic addressing for tools, and stream deduplication.

code.finding: Biome requires multi-line formatting for long console.warn() calls; single-line form fails the formatter check.
getByText('Main chat') matched chat-session-name spans inside hidden modal mounts. Fixed by targeting .session-item[data-session-key='main'] .first() to hit the visible sidebar item.
SessionHeader is mounted twice: toolbar mount with showName=false (always hidden), and modal mount inside #chatMoreModal (hidden by default). Both contain chat-session-name spans.

workflow: biome --write doesn't always persist to disk when run via Bash tool in the session; safer to apply the formatting change directly via Edit tool.
When a release prep commit is already on remote but the tag was never pushed, re-tagging and pushing the tag is sufficient — no need to re-run the prepare script.
git rebase with stash can leave the working tree in a confusing state (rebase complete but stash pop conflicts staged); git status is unreliable as a quick sanity check in this scenario.
Playwright getByText matches hidden elements; prefer attribute-based selectors (data-session-key, data-label-text) when asserting sidebar items are visible.

friction: biome --write appeared to succeed but did not persist to disk, requiring a manual Edit tool application and a second verification pass.
Release tagging sequence was disrupted by new remote commits arriving mid-flow, triggering a failed rebase+stash cycle that required an abort and clean restart.
The release was interrupted mid-flow by the user merging a new PR, requiring a full state cleanup (reset, unstage, pull) before re-attempting.
git pull failed due to unstaged changes in apps/ios/GraphQL/Schema/schema.graphqls, which was an unrelated local modification blocking routine pulls.
The Playwright locator strategy (getByText) was fragile against duplicate hidden DOM elements, requiring investigation of the full component mount tree to diagnose.
open_items: apps/ios/GraphQL/Schema/schema.graphqls has a local modification (added kind, warnings, ContextWarning fields) that was committed in the final push but may need a corresponding iOS client update.
The release 20260328.03 was tagged on a commit that predates the last merged PR — the changelog may be missing that PR's entry.
The forum topic isolation implementation referenced in discussion #442 should be verified with integration tests, not just the code review comment.
